stories.title: Giannis Trade Official — Heat Land the Greek Freak, Celtics Miss Out

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: OFFICIAL: Giannis Antetokounmpo is a Miami Heat. Bucks get Herro, Ware, Jaquez, Jakucionis + No. 13 pick + 2 firsts. 13 seasons. 2 MVPs. 1 ring. A new era begins. #MiamiHeat #Bucks

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: The Celtics offered Jaylen Brown + 2 picks for Giannis. Milwaukee said no. Miami wins the sweepstakes. What a night to be an NBA fan. 🍿 #NBA #Celtics #MiamiHeat

facebook.long_form:
🚨 IT'S OFFICIAL: GIANNIS IS A MIAMI HEAT 🚨

After weeks of speculation, the Milwaukee Bucks have traded Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Miami Heat in one of the biggest deals in NBA history.

Here's what each team gets:

🔥 MIAMI HEAT RECEIVE:
• Giannis Antetokounmpo
• Bobby Portis

📦 MILWAUKEE BUCKS RECEIVE:
• Tyler Herro
• Kel'el Ware
• Jaime Jaquez Jr.
• Kasparas Jakucionis
• No. 13 pick (2026 Draft)
• Two additional first-round picks

The Boston Celtics had offered Jaylen Brown plus two first-round picks — and still came up short. Milwaukee chose youth and draft capital over an established star.

For Giannis, it's the end of a 13-year era in Milwaukee — one that included two MVP awards and the 2021 NBA Championship. He leaves as the greatest Buck since Kareem Abdul-Jabbar.

In Miami, he joins Bam Adebayo to form one of the most formidable frontcourts in the league. The Heat are immediately back in the championship conversation.

What do you think — did Miami or Milwaukee win this trade?

article.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo Traded to Miami Heat: Bucks End 13-Year Era in Blockbuster Deal

Giannis Trade — Heat

- Milwaukee receives: Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis, No. 13 overall pick (2026 Draft), plus two additional first-round picks
- Heat also receive Bobby Portis from Milwaukee
- Celtics offered: Jaylen Brown + 2 first-round picks — rejected
- Giannis is 13-year Bucks veteran; won 2 MVPs, 2021 NBA title, 2021 Finals MVP
- Bucks led by Milwaukee era: 2 MVPs (2019, 2020), 2021 championship over Phoenix Suns
- Trade announced night before the 2026 NBA Draft (June 22 into June 23)
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple outlets confirmed)
**Source:** Yahoo Sports draft preview
- Heat now have the No. 13 pick (received from Bucks)
- Draft runs June 23–24, Barclays Center, 8 PM ET ABC/ESPN

stories.title: NBA Draft Night — Dybantsa Expected No. 1, Wizards Set on BYU Star

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: NBA Draft Night is HERE. AJ Dybantsa — 25.5 PPG, 51% shooting at BYU — is set to go No. 1 to the Wizards. He didn't work out for anyone. "They know what I can do." 8 PM ET ABC/ESPN. #NBADraft

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Wild twist: the Miami Heat are now picking No. 13 tonight — the Bucks pick they got in the Giannis trade. The Heat enter draft night as the most interesting team to watch. 👀 #NBADraft #MiamiHeat

facebook.long_form:
🏀 IT'S NBA DRAFT NIGHT AND THE WHOLE LEAGUE JUST SHIFTED 🏀

The 2026 NBA Draft tips off tonight at 8 PM ET from Barclays Center in Brooklyn — and for the first time in weeks, we actually know who's picking where.

Here's your cheat sheet for tonight:

📋 TOP EXPECTED PICKS:
• No. 1 — AJ Dybantsa (BYU) → Washington Wizards
• No. 2 — Darryn Peterson (Kansas) → Utah Jazz
• No. 3 — Cameron Boozer → expected to follow
• No. 13 — Miami Heat (received from Bucks in Giannis trade!)
• No. 24 — New York Knicks
• No. 31 — New York Knicks (via Washington)

The storyline of the night is AJ Dybantsa. The 6-foot-9 BYU freshman averaged 25.5 points and shot 51% from the floor. He didn't work out for a single team before the draft — saying "I don't gotta work out. They know what I can do."

That kind of confidence. Tonight, the Wizards almost certainly make it official.

Tune in tonight at 8 PM ET on ABC and ESPN!

article.headline: NBA Draft Night 2026: AJ Dybantsa on the Clock as Giannis Trade Reshapes the Board

stories.title: Austin Reaves Opt-Out Watch — Lakers' $241M Deadline Looms June 29

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Austin Reaves expected to opt out by June 29. Lakers offer: 5yr/$241M. Nets offer: 4yr/$178.5M. His take: won't come down solely to dollars. Multiple sources expect him to stay in LA. #LakeShow

facebook.long_form:
⏰ THE AUSTIN REAVES COUNTDOWN IS ON ⏰

Austin Reaves has until June 29 — just six days away — to decide whether to opt out of his $14.9 million player option and enter free agency. All signs point to him opting out.

Here's what the market looks like:

🟣💛 LOS ANGELES LAKERS: 5-year offer, up to $241 million
⚫ BROOKLYN NETS: 4-year offer, approximately $178.5 million

That's a $62.5 million difference in favor of the Lakers.

But Reaves has made clear: "It won't come down solely to dollars." Multiple sources around the league expect him to re-sign with Los Angeles, where he averaged 23.3 points, 5.5 assists, and 4.7 rebounds this season.

The real question: does Reaves bet on the Lakers remaining championship contenders — especially with LeBron James and the supporting cast they've built?

June 29 is circled on every Lakers fan's calendar.

article.headline: Austin Reaves Opt-Out Watch: Lakers' $241M Offer vs. Nets' $178.5M as June 29 Looms

stories.title: Ja Morant Trade Market Shifts — Heat Off the Board, Who Steps Up?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The Heat just took Giannis — so who trades for Ja Morant? Miami is off the board. Suns, Kings, and Pelicans step up as leading suitors. Post-draft market opens NOW. 🔥 #NBA #Grizzlies

facebook.long_form:
🔀 THE JA MORANT TRADE MARKET JUST CHANGED OVERNIGHT 🔀

For weeks, the Miami Heat were considered the Plan B for a Ja Morant trade — in case they missed out on Giannis Antetokounmpo.

Well, Miami got Giannis. So the Morant market just reshuffled entirely.

Here's the updated landscape:

🔥 Phoenix Suns — now the leading candidate
🏀 Sacramento Kings — in conversation
🐝 New Orleans Pelicans — monitoring situation
🐺 Minnesota Timberwolves — also interested

Ja Morant is 27 years old and averaging 24.4 points per game. He's owed $42.1 million in 2026-27 and $44.8 million in 2027-28. Memphis holds No. 3 overall pick tonight, which could be packaged in a deal.

The post-draft Morant market opens now. Which team lands the high-flying Grizzlies star?

article.headline: Ja Morant Trade Market Reshuffles: Heat Out After Giannis Deal, Suns Move to the Front

stories.title: Knicks at the Draft Table — Championship Team Makes Picks No. 24 and No. 31

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: The NBA champs enter draft night with picks No. 24 and No. 31. But the real Knicks offseason work? Brunson extension. OG Anunoby. Karl-Anthony Towns. That's what running it back looks like. 🏆🗽 #NewYorkKnicks

facebook.long_form:
🗽 THE CHAMPIONS ENTER THE DRAFT — BUT THEIR EYES ARE ELSEWHERE 🗽

The New York Knicks are NBA champions. And tonight, they'll pick in the draft — at No. 24 and No. 31.

Not bad. But that's the price of winning: you draft late.

Here's the real Knicks offseason agenda:

✅ No. 1: Jalen Brunson extension talks
✅ No. 2: Re-sign OG Anunoby
✅ No. 3: Bring back Karl-Anthony Towns
✅ No. 4: Manage the luxury tax reality

The Knicks won their first championship in 54 years this June. Two million fans showed up for the parade. Now it's time to build the roster to run it back.

Tonight's picks at No. 24 and No. 31? That's depth. The core is locked in — the front office just needs to keep it together.

Can the Knicks do what only a handful of franchises have done in the modern era? Win back-to-back titles?

The work starts tonight.

article.headline: Knicks Enter Draft Night as Champions: Picks No. 24 and No. 31 as Bigger Offseason Priorities Loom
